questions.question: If an aircraft burns 0.12 liters of fuel per minute, how much fuel will it burn on a 1 hour and 17 minute flight?

You're flying a Small UAS with a maximum airspeed of 30 MPH. Your mission site is located 6 SM due south from the geometric center of a major sports stadium. A 5 NM radius TFR is active for an event at the stadium. If you were to fly full "throttle" directly towards the stadium, how long would it take to enter restricted airspace? [[NEXT_LINE]] KABC 120855Z 12007KT 10SM CLR 23/17 A2969

The Blue Angels are about to make an appearance at the Super Bowl! The singer won't start until they receive the go-ahead from the Blue Angel coordinator at the stadium. If the national anthem takes 1 minute 59 seconds to sing, and the jets fly formation at 230 KTAS, how far away will they be when their coordinator gives the singer the signal, assuming calm winds and a straight flight path?

ATC wants you to descend now to cross the NIIFA intersection at 11,000' MSL. Your navigation system estimates 9 minutes 42 seconds to get there. If you're currently at FL410 and have an average ground speed of 700 KM/HR, what is your descent rate?
Reference the previous question. How far are you from NIIFA as you pass 18,000'?
questions.context: Referenced question 20: ATC wants you to descend now to cross the NIIFA intersection at 11,000' MSL. Your navigation system estimates 9 minutes 42 seconds to get there. If you're currently at FL410 and have an average ground speed of 700 KM/HR, what is your descent rate?

questions.question: At 80 knots, how long would it take you to fly 522 SM?
You must be able to fly and descend to your destination, climb to the diversion altitude, and arrive at your alternate with 45 minutes of cruise reserve fuel. How much time can you hold at DEPDY?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 36-40: You are in a hold at DEPDY at 10,000' MSL with 5,800 LBS of jet fuel. Your destination is KLGA (21' MSL) on a true course of 052° and 45 NM away. Your alternate is KABE (394' MSL) on a true course of 277° and 72 NM from KLGA. The average winds aloft are 320° at 30 knots. Your average level flight performance is 250 KTAS and 3,600 LBS/hr of fuel burn. Your average descent performance is 1,500 FPM, 250 KTAS, and 1,200 LBS/hr of fuel burn. During a missed approach followed by a diversion you would climb to 15,000' MSL with climb performance of 3,500 FPM, 250 KTAS, and 8,000 LBS/hr of fuel burn.

questions.question: How much total fuel do you burn while descending (include both LGA and ABE)?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 36-40: You are in a hold at DEPDY at 10,000' MSL with 5,800 LBS of jet fuel. Your destination is KLGA (21' MSL) on a true course of 052° and 45 NM away. Your alternate is KABE (394' MSL) on a true course of 277° and 72 NM from KLGA. The average winds aloft are 320° at 30 knots. Your average level flight performance is 250 KTAS and 3,600 LBS/hr of fuel burn. Your average descent performance is 1,500 FPM, 250 KTAS, and 1,200 LBS/hr of fuel burn. During a missed approach followed by a diversion you would climb to 15,000' MSL with climb performance of 3,500 FPM, 250 KTAS, and 8,000 LBS/hr of fuel burn.

questions.question: You are practicing instrument flying skills with your flight instructor. The instructor tells you to turn 360° at a standard rate of 3° per second. Upon the completion of the turn, the instructor looks at the ground and determines that the winds aloft pushed you 1.1 nm away from your original point. How strong are the winds aloft?

ATC needs you to stay level at 3100' MSL until beginning your final descent to land at a runway which is currently 6.5 nm away. You maintain 75 knots groundspeed and plan a 3.2° descent angle. If the touchdown zone elevation is 1400' MSL, how long will it take before you start descent?
After flying 190 minutes at 190 knots, how far have you traveled?
A pilot in the Message Drop event flies at 90 knots over Runway 22 (6,701' long) in Janesville. The targets are located at each end of the runway. How long will the pilot have between the two drops?
How many liters of Avgas do you burn on this flight?
questions.context: Use the following information for questions 36-40. Fuel on board: 74 gallons usable. Outbound true course: 340°. Cruise: 148 KTAS, 45.4 liters per hour. Variation: 9°E. Deviation: +3°. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: While planning for a practice VFR Navigation flight, you figure your time to fly 1 statute mile to be 19 seconds. What is your indicated airspeed on a calm wind, standard day at 3,500' MSL?

You are standing at the threshold of runway 16R as a Gulfstream G650 flies overhead. At an approach speed of 139 knots and a length of 99 feet 9 inches, how long does it take from the nose passing over to the tail passing over?
Referencing the previous question, how long will it take to do four full turns around that point?
questions.context: Referenced question 11: While making a turn around a point at 85 knots, the distance traveled in one full turn was 4.4 NM. How far from the point was maintained on average?

questions.question: How many pounds of jet fuel have been burned between two checkpoints 188 NM apart if the aircraft is flying at 433 knots with a total burn rate of 4,160 pounds per hour?

You enter oceanic airspace over KEEKA at 1553 Zulu. ATC assigns you a cruise speed of Mach 0.78 at FL370 burning 6,000 pounds of fuel per hour. ATC asks what time you will be able to climb to FL380. Your computer shows that you weigh 161,000 pounds and that FL370 is your maximum altitude currently. After some research you note that at 150,000 pounds you'll be able to climb to FL380. What time do you tell ATC?
How long will the aircraft be level in cruise?
questions.context: Use the following information to solve questions 36-40. An aircraft leaves their departure airport (Elev. 1,076') on a true course of 160° for 114 NM to their destination airport (Elev. 661'). Departure winds are 185° at 12 knots; destination winds are calm. Winds aloft at a cruise altitude of 7,500' MSL are 285° at 21 knots. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: How many gallons of fuel are required if a 30-minute VFR reserve (cruise fuel burn) is included?
questions.context: Use the following information to solve questions 36-40. An aircraft leaves their departure airport (Elev. 1,076') on a true course of 160° for 114 NM to their destination airport (Elev. 661'). Departure winds are 185° at 12 knots; destination winds are calm. Winds aloft at a cruise altitude of 7,500' MSL are 285° at 21 knots. [[TABLE]]

questions.question: You are flying at FL340 and ISA+7, and ATC assigns you the following: "Cross COMAC at 1748Z, current time 1733Z, after COMAC maintain Mach 0.79." Looking at your GPS, COMAC is 124 nm away on a 347° true course. If the winds aloft are 270° at 47 knots, what Mach number should you fly to COMAC?

While flying along at your cruise altitude of 37,000' ATC tells you, "Area of moderate to severe turbulence, 12 o'clock, 10 miles and will continue for the next 50 miles." How long do you have until you enter the area of turbulence if you have an IAS of 260 kts and the OAT is showing -55°C? You also have a 121-mph headwind.
Reference the previous question. How long would you be in the area of turbulence from the time that you entered it?
questions.context: Referenced question 28: While flying along at your cruise altitude of 37,000' ATC tells you, "Area of moderate to severe turbulence, 12 o'clock, 10 miles and will continue for the next 50 miles." How long do you have until you enter the area of turbulence if you have an IAS of 260 kts and the OAT is showing -55°C? You also have a 121-mph headwind.

questions.question: Cruising at Mach 0.73, how long does it take to fly 52 NM at FL340? Assume ISA temperature, wind of 235° at 49 knots and true course of 267°.

While flying at FL350 and M0.78 at ISA+14°C, you are estimating COMPC at 19:23:00, and it is 247 nm away. ATC needs you to cross COMPC at 19:25:12. Current time is 18:51:00. What speed (in Mach) should you fly to comply with the clearance? Assume calm wind.
Referencing the previous question, how long will it take the fueler to fill your tanks with the required amount of fuel plus 750 extra pounds of fuel that your captain asked for?
questions.context: Referenced question 39: In Loreta, Mexico the fuel truck pumps fuel at 19.5 liters/second. If your dispatch release indicates that you need 18,700 pounds of fuel total in your B737 and you landed with 5,430 pounds, how much fuel do you need to tell the fueler to fill up your jet with assuming Jet-A weighs 6.75 lbs/gallon?

questions.question: You wish to check your actual groundspeed against your flight planned groundspeed. You planned to have a groundspeed of 102 knots, but you see that you crossed 3 statute miles in 1 min 28 seconds. How far off your planned groundspeed are you?

Driving to work is a nuisance. You must travel 15 km straight ahead before making a right turn and traveling 8 km before making a final left turn and driving an additional 17 km. How much quicker would it be to fly direct in a helicopter with a ground speed of 70 knots as opposed to driving at 99 km/h?

You put a fresh set of batteries into your headset giving it a capacity of 2,400 mAh. There is 432 nm remaining on your flight. True course is 114°. The winds aloft are 050° at 21 KTS and your TAS is 90 knots. The headset drains at 80 mA and you accidently leave your headset on for an additional 2 hours after landing. How much battery remains for your next flight?
Your C172 cruise speed is 105 KTAS. With an average wind of 315° at 28 KTS, how long would it take you to fly from JFK to LAX? The distance is 2473 sm on a true course of 275°.
Earth is 24,901 SM around at the equator. How long would it take you to fly around the earth nonstop at an average ground speed of 250 KTS?

From the previous question, it took you 73 minutes to get to that point. You determine you are 7.6 minutes behind schedule, how many knots do you need to increase to make up your deficit by your destination?
questions.context: Referenced question 3: You are currently 118 NM into your 300 SM trip. You decide to do a position check and determine that you are currently 3.6 SM off course. How many degrees do you need to turn to proceed direct to your destination?

questions.question: If the truck is driving between two cities 275 SM apart and leaves at noon, what time would you have to leave in your airplane to arrive at the same time?
questions.context: Referenced question 9: You are flying parallel to the highway and are displeased that it seems like a truck is keeping up with you. If you are flying due west at 90 KTS and the winds are 310° at 20 KTS who is faster assuming the truck is doing the 65 MPH speed limit?

questions.question: What will be the total time from Pine Bluffs to the turn and then reaching Cascade Fields?
questions.context: Use the following information to answer questions 29 to 34: [[NEXT_LINE]] Due to deteriorating weather conditions, you are forced to file an alternate airport for your final 466 SM leg from Pine Bluffs (82V) to Sand Springs (8U1). [[NEXT_LINE]] You choose Cascade Fields (D55), which is 120° from Pine Bluffs at 272 NM. [[NEXT_LINE]] You plan for 161 KTAS on a 051° true course to Sand Springs and you burn avgas at 14.3 GPH while holding 65.4 total gallons. [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds are 310° at 31 knots. [[DIVIDER]] Referenced question 31: At your time to turn you decide to fly to Cascade Fields. What will be your true course?

questions.question: It is exactly 112.8 KM between Wispy Willows Airpark and Rocky Shores Municipal airport. You estimate an average ground speed of 128 MPH. How long will this flight take?
You just purchased a 1981 C-172P with 27,667 hours on the airframe. How many seconds is this?

You are at the flight dispatch desk hoping to start your lesson early because you have a class the next morning. Unfortunately, your assigned aircraft is still out flying. You look at their ADS-B data at 20:12 Zulu and they are showing headed back to the airport at a ground speed of 94 KTS. If they are 32 NM away from the airport, what time will they return? Assume constant GS.
The winds are reported from 240° at 17 knots while you are on final for runway 22. If you are 14 KM away how long will it take you to reach the runway if you are flying 90 KTAS?

Sal dropped a 1-quart can of aviation grade engine oil onto the floor, puncturing a hole into it. If it began leaking at a rate of four drops every two seconds, how long will it take for the can to drip empty if each drop is 0.25 mL?

You and your friend decide to go to Las Vegas for the weekend. You depart from Mesquite (70 NM away) on a course of 219° and 105 KTAS. If your friend wants to arrive at the same time as you (1548Z), and he is departing from St. George on a course of 223° and 98 NM, what time would he have to depart if he wants to cruise at 105 KTAS? (Wind is 265° at 23 KTS)
You wish to fly from KDYL to KUKT, which is a distance of 22 nautical miles. How long will this flight take if your aircraft can maintain a ground speed of 63 statute miles per hour?
Ground speed is 452 knots. How far will you travel in 17 minutes?
Having developed a fuel imbalance of 1,200 pounds, how long must you crossfeed to balance your fuel tanks? The total fuel burn is 6,700 pounds per hour.
Using those winds as you turn to the next leg of your flight with a course of 140° how long do you expect it will take to fly the 25 nm leg?
questions.context: Referenced question 7: You start out by flying a 210° heading but realize you have drifted 5° right of your intended course. To correct you turn to a heading of 190° and hold a 2° right wind correction angle. If your planned airspeed is 125 kts what are the winds aloft?

questions.question: It's a beautiful sunny afternoon and you're out for a short flight in your Piper Cub with your friend. Your friend asks how fast you are going, as the cars appear to be passing you. You notice that it takes you 1 min and 32 seconds to cover 1 statute mile. What is your ground speed?

You have been hired to do a flyover of a stadium with your vintage F-86 on a blistering 95°F summer day. You are orbiting at low altitude 5 nm from the stadium when you get the radio call marking 5 minutes until the flyover. If you are going to make the approach and pass at Mach 0.90, when should you depart your orbit point? Assume calm winds.

choices.text: 3 minutes, 52 seconds after the radio call
4 minutes, 11 seconds after the radio call
4 minutes, 31 seconds after the radio call
4 minutes, 47 seconds after the radio call

questions.correctChoice: 4 minutes, 31 seconds after the radio call

questions.question: How long will it take to fly 1,900 kilometers at Mach 0.62 with an OAT of -40°F?
An aircraft flying a straight path southeasterly at 97 knots crosses radial 042 and then 1 minute 42 seconds later crosses radial 057 from the same VOR. How much distance was flown in that time?
What is the ground speed of a plane that covers 47 nautical miles in 20 minutes?

You are designing a fireworks display, and the launch area is 1.2 sm from the main viewing area. You need to synchronize the sound of the fireworks with the music that will be playing from speakers in the viewing area. What is the time delay between when the fireworks burst and when the sound reaches the viewing area? The night of the show is forecast to be 78°F.

You've been signed off for your tailwheel endorsement, and now you want to fly the Cub solo. You're tired of the pattern, so you decide to fly out and back. Your chosen course is 340° with average winds of 270° at 24 knots. Your true airspeed will be an average of 70 miles per hour. You paid for an extra 2 hours of solo flight time. How long do you need to fly out to complete the flight in exactly 2.0 hours, including the taxi? Assume 0.2 hours of total taxi time.
How long will it take you to fly that distance at a ground speed of 174 KMPH?
questions.context: Referenced question 19: You are inbound on the 273° radial to CZI VOR. You are to fly the 20 DME arc northbound. How far have you flown on the arc if you exit at the 345° radial?

questions.question: Usain Bolt holds the world record for the 100 meter dash at 9.58 SECS. How fast is this?
It takes 51 seconds to travel 18,228'. What is your groundspeed?
How long will it take an aircraft to fly 194 NM at an average speed of 108 MPH?
You want to determine your ground speed by timing how long it takes you to fly over 1 sm square farm fields. If you cross two fields in 1 minute 36 seconds what is your ground speed?
With a ground speed of 137 MPH and a headwind of 15 KTS, how many miles will you travel in 2 hours and 27 minutes?
At the 2016 Olympics, the U.S. swim team took the gold medal in the 800 meter relay event in 7 minutes, 0.66 seconds. What speed did the team average?
When Luke Aikins jumped out of an aircraft without a parachute, it took him 2 MINS 09 SECS to cover 25,000' before safely landing in the safety net. What was his average speed during the freefall?

Your C172 has a steady electrical load of 23.2 amps. Halfway through your flight, you lose your alternator and are running off your 35 amp-hour battery. Assuming you are able to shed 8.7 amps, how many minutes can you fly before depleting your battery?

Riding along in the exit row of an Airbus A330, you wonder what local time it will be when you flyover New York City. You glance at the flight information screen and see that you left at 16:32 Zurich time which is GMT+2, are currently cruising at Mach 0.83 at FL380 (ISA-4) and have flown for 41 minutes so far with 6,022 km remaining. What day and local time will it be when you flyover New York City, which is GMT-4?
Referencing the previous question, how long will it take you to do four full turns around a point?
questions.context: Referenced question 24: You are doing a turn around a point and you are maintaining 90 knots and in one full circle you travel 2.6 nm. How far from the point are you maintaining on average?

questions.question: Your best friend just got tickets for the both of you to see the Green Bay Packers take on the Minnesota Vikings in Green Bay. He asks that you fly from Green Bay to pick him up from Traverse City Airport in your spacious Cessna 152. If you want to arrive back in Green Bay 2 hours before kickoff, when is the latest you should take off from Green Bay? (Kick off is 15:30 CST; disregard time spent on the ground in TVC)
questions.context: Use the following info for questions 30-32: [[NEXT_LINE]] Dist. = 110 nm Magnetic Course (GRB-TVC) = 083 TAS = 121 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Magnetic Winds = 310@35 knots Fuel Flow = 5.5 gph [[NEXT_LINE]] Assume the same performance for climb and descent.

questions.question: While planning for this practice VFR Navigation flight, you figure your time to fly 1 statute mile to be 33 seconds. What is your indicated airspeed on a calm wind, standard day at 4,500' MSL?

ATC gives you a crossing restriction to cross the JDB VOR at exactly 85 knots. You are currently at 100 knots and 2500 rpm. To properly cool your engine you must only reduce 100 rpm for every one minute of flight. For every 100 rpm you reduce, you slow your airspeed by 3 knots (assume airspeed loss is instantaneous). How far from the VOR must you begin to reduce power in order to pull the last 100 rpm exactly as you are crossing the VOR?
How long will it take you to fly 1.5 NM at 4,500' MSL while indicating 95 knots under standard conditions and calm wind?

Assuming an average true airspeed of 108 knots from the beginning and average winds of 040@28 miles per hour, what time would you need to be underway in order to cross over the center of the TFR before it becomes active?
questions.context: In the preflight briefing for your upcoming 328 statute mile cross country trip you learn that there will be a TFR directly under your true course of 332 degrees. The center of the TFR will be located 185 nautical miles along your route. The TFR is 5 nautical miles across, from the surface to 7,000 feet, and will be active from 1400Z to 1600Z. Use this information to answer the next 7 questions.

questions.question: Unfortunately, through a bit of bureaucratic kerfuffle, one of the enforcement agencies interprets the size of the TFR to be larger than originally briefed to you. When you pass just to the left of what you thought was the boundary, the alarm bells go off and they scramble two interceptors. The first interceptor is a fighter jet that will fly from his base directly to where he thinks you'll be at the point of intercept (since you are flying in a straight line). The second interceptor is a helicopter that will depart from a different base and do the same. The helicopter has a cruise speed of 85 knots and has to travel 70 statute miles on a course of 225° to get to his intercept point. Wind at his altitude is 050@15 knots. How long will it take for him to reach his intercept point?
questions.context: In the preflight briefing for your upcoming 328 statute mile cross country trip you learn that there will be a TFR directly under your true course of 332 degrees. The center of the TFR will be located 185 nautical miles along your route. The TFR is 5 nautical miles across, from the surface to 7,000 feet, and will be active from 1400Z to 1600Z. Use this information to answer the next 7 questions.

questions.question: What is the total flight time in cruise?
questions.context: Use the following information to answer questions 20 through 23. [[NEXT_LINE]] Wind (true): 285° at 27 knots at cruise, calm winds for climb and descent [[NEXT_LINE]] Standard temperature and pressure [[NEXT_LINE]] Variation: 7W [[NEXT_LINE]] Magnetic heading: 159° [[NEXT_LINE]] Total distance: 206 statute miles [[NEXT_LINE]] Total fuel burn: 14.48 US gallons [[NEXT_LINE]] Departure elevation: 1,350' [[NEXT_LINE]] Destination elevation: 820' [[NEXT_LINE]] Climb: 10m15s and 2.27 US gallons of fuel, 84 knot groundspeed [[NEXT_LINE]] Cruise: 127.5 knot groundspeed [[NEXT_LINE]] Descent: 8m21s and 0.86 US gallons of fuel, 800 fpm descent rate, 122 knot groundspeed

questions.question: Frank fuels his aircraft at a rate of 0.33 imperial gallons/second and Steve fuels his at 3,845 liters/hour. How long will it take each to pump 120 gallons of fuel?
Referencing the previous question, how many miles would you fly during that time if the wind is 240° true at 74 knots. Your magnetic course is 004° and the variation is 14° W. OAT is -64°C.
questions.context: Referenced question 25: You enter oceanic airspace over KEEKA at 1553 zulu. ATC assigns you a cruise speed of M .78 at FL370 burning 6,000 pounds of fuel per hour. ATC asks at what time you will be able to climb and maintain FL380. You look in the FMC and see that you weigh 161,000 pounds and that FL 370 is your maximum altitude currently. You research and notice that you can only weigh 150,000 pounds to reach FL 380. What time do you tell ATC?

questions.question: How long will it take to fly 1.7 nm on a true course of 256° at an IAS of 144 knots at 3,500' pressure altitude and a temperature of +24°C if the wind is from 140° true at 18 knots?
When Charles Lindberg crossed the Atlantic Ocean in 1927, he flew for 33.5 hours and traveled 3610 statute miles. What was his average speed?

A few years later in 1938, a fellow named Howard decided to show off his fast airplane and flew around the world. His first leg was from Floyd Bennett airport in New York, NY to the same airport in Paris where Lindberg landed. This leg covered 3641 statute miles in a mere 16 hours and 38 minutes. What was his average ground speed?
You are cruising at a blistering 73 knots ground speed in your C150. How long will it take to fly 2 nautical miles?
After flying for 2 hours 37 minutes at a ground speed of 190 knots, how far have you flown?
At a ground speed of 283 km/hr, how many NM are covered in 8 minutes and 6 seconds? _______________________________ Nautical Miles (to the tenth of a nautical mile)
.38 hours equals how many seconds?
6.1 hours is how many seconds?
You travel at 337 km/hr for 3 hour 13 minutes. What distance did you cover?
Four hundredths of an hour is how many seconds? ______________________ (whole number only)
A trip is 457 nm long and you're traveling at 187 kts. How long will it take?
You have been flying for 2 hours and 57 minutes at a ground speed of 169 kts. How far have you flown?
Assuming a straight out departure and straight in landing, the total time of the trip is:
questions.context: Use the following to answer questions 27 - 31. You're departing Hometown airport (elevation 2280') with a 400 fpm rate of climb to a cruise altitude of 6500 feet. Your groundspeed in the climb is 85 kts and in cruise it is 120 kts. You descend to 1000 feet above destination airport (elevation 1700) with a groundspeed of 140 kts and a de scent rate of 650 fpm. Your fuel consumption is 11 gph in climb, 9.5 gph in cruise and 7 gph in descent. You are carrying 27 gallons of usable fuel. The total distance of the flight is 295 nm.

questions.question: The total elapsed time from the beginning of cruise to the end of descent is
questions.context: Use the following to answer questions 27 - 31. You're departing Hometown airport (elevation 2280') with a 400 fpm rate of climb to a cruise altitude of 6500 feet. Your groundspeed in the climb is 85 kts and in cruise it is 120 kts. You descend to 1000 feet above destination airport (elevation 1700) with a groundspeed of 140 kts and a de scent rate of 650 fpm. Your fuel consumption is 11 gph in climb, 9.5 gph in cruise and 7 gph in descent. You are carrying 27 gallons of usable fuel. The total distance of the flight is 295 nm.

questions.question: A groundspeed check shows that we're covering.28 sm every 13 seconds. We've traveled 258 nm. What is our GS?
How long will it take to travel 315 NM at a groundspeed of 167 kts?
How long will it take to travel.75 NM at a groundspeed of 121 kts?
How long will it take to travel 372 statute miles covering 9.5 km per minute?

The distance between two checkpoints Is 18 NM. You cross the first checkpoint at 10:11. At 10:17 you cross checkpoint #2. The remaining trip distance is 175 NM. What will be your ETA based on your computed groundspeed?
How far will you travel in 4:09:00 at an average groundspeed of 121 kts?
At a ground speed of 184 knots, what will be the time required to cover 288 nautical miles?
If you fly at 107 knots for 107 minutes, how far will you have travelled?

choices.text: 220 statute miles Two aircraft are both on final for the same runway at a busy, tower-controlled airport. The controller knows that the first aircraft will fly 144 mph after crossing a point 7 nautical miles from the end of the runway. The second aircraft is behind the first, also on a final approach. Use this information to answer

questions.correctChoice: 220 statute miles Two aircraft are both on final for the same runway at a busy, tower-controlled airport. The controller knows that the first aircraft will fly 144 mph after crossing a point 7 nautical miles from the end of the runway. The second aircraft is behind the first, also on a final approach. Use this information to answer

questions.question: the following questions: If the winds are calm, how long will it take the first aircraft to travel the last 5 statute miles of the approach?

While in a steady climb late at night, you discover a need to know your climb rate. Unfortunately, inky shadows have obscured your VSI. Undeterred, you decide to use your flight computer to solve your problem. But before you do that, what is your ground speed if you have covered 200 nautical miles in just 78 minutes?
If the airspeed is 163 mph, what is the time required to cover 272 statute miles?

If you had been able to fly from Home to A (85 statute miles) in 37 minutes and 45 seconds, you would suspect that the winds were different from the forecast. You would suspect this because your ground speed was:
If you have travelled 284 statute miles in 3 hours and 8 minutes, what is your ground speed?
A pilot files for 12 minutes and 17 seconds at a ground speed of 132 mph. What is the distance flown?
You are flying at an airspeed of 431 knots, how many nm are you covering each minute?
Convert 37.3 minutes to hundredths of an hour.
You have an average ground speed of 134 knots, and it took you 77 seconds to fly from the outer marker to the middle marker. What is the distance between the two markers?
How long will it take to travel 441 NM at a groundspeed of 424 km/h?
How long will it take to travel 380 statute miles?
A pilot files for 35 minutes and 48 seconds at a ground speed of 144 mph. What is the distance flown?
You depart an airport with an elevation of 840'. At an average rate of climb of 975 ft./min., how long will it take to climb to an indicated altitude of 12,500' at a groundspeed of 88 kts?
How far will you travel in 4:17:00 at an average groundspeed of 151 mph?
How long will it take to travel 4253 ft. at a groundspeed of 98 kts.- ____________________________________ (whole number) Write your answer on the answer sheet

The distance between two checkpoints is 22 NM. You cross the first checkpoint at 1019. At 1027 you cross the second checkpoint. The remaining trip distance is 154 NM. What will your ETA be based on your computed groundspeed? ____________________________________ (hr: min) Write your answer on the answer sheet

After turning on course to Salt Lake City, the company asks for your estimated time of arrival. When you turned, your wristwatch, which is set to central daylight time (UTC-5,) read 10:18pm. FedEx needs the information in UTC since that's all they use. If it's 278 nm from Casper to Salt Lake City, what will you give as your ETA?

You have been chosen to do the fly-over of the stadium for Game 1 of the World Series. You'll have to time the fly-over to coincide with the end of the National Anthem, so you decide to circle around a point due North of the stadium. Assuming it takes 1 min 20 sec to sing the Star Spangled Banner, how far away from the stadium should you be circling when the singing begins, if you will be flying at a TAS of 200 kts with winds of 210@14 kts?
You are in a Boeing 737 traveling at 460 mph. You have 164 km to go. How much time do you have left on your trip?

true course is 61° and you will cover 206 statute miles. The wind is from a steady 310° at 26 knots for all phases of flight and you will cruise at 5,500' MSL. Climb: 100 KTAS 800 FPM 14.3 GPH Cruise: 120 KTAS --- 12.0 GPH Descent: 130 KTAS 650 FPM 7.1 GPH What is your time to climb to 5,500 feet?

You are flying at 6,500 MSL with an altimeter setting of 30.22". The outside temperature is 21°C and you are indicating 133 mph. If the wind is 330° true at 18 knots, how long will a 429 km flight take on a magnetic course of 038°? The local variation is 5° west. (Write your answer your answer on the answer sheet in hh: mm: ss to the nearest second.)

You are planning your climb profile. From the airport (elevation 1300 ft.) you fly out on a TC of 040°. You fly 105 KTAS & 1800 fpm to 11,000 ft. Then you fly 125 KTAS & 1100 fpm to FL180. Upon reaching FL180 you turn to a TC of 120° and continue to climb at 140 KTAS & 600 fpm to FL270. Average winds are 060@18 knots. How long will it take to reach FL270?
It takes 2 hours 38 minutes to fly 380 SM. What is your speed in mph?
Your best five kilometer (5K) run is 21:42. If a Cessna 172 above cruises at 140 knots (groundspeed), how much sooner would the Cessna 172 finish a five kilometer race?
You fly 1.8 km in 25 seconds. What is your groundspeed?
5616 minutes is how many days? (Provide an answer to nearest 1/10 of a day)

You are departing from Camas Municipal Airport, WA heading north to Seattle-Tacoma International Airport, WA. To check your groundspeed you use the few sections of land (1 section of land = 1 statute mile) that exist. If you cross 5 sections of land in 3 minutes and 36 seconds, how fast are you going?
For calculation, you use a straight line distance from The Dalles to Seattle, a groundspeed of 118 km/hr, and an assumption of an extra 5.2 minutes to deviate. What is the total time enroute?
questions.context: John wants to fly directly from The Dalles, OR to Seattle, WA. However, the only problem is that there is a 12,000 foot mountain the way, Mt. Adams. There are calm winds aloft. He plans on flying the 118 nautical mile, true course of 324° route as follows: [[NEXT_LINE]] 1st Fly 20 nautical miles on the original true course. [[NEXT_LINE]] 2nd Fly on a true course of 310° for 93 km. This puts you abeam Mt. Adams. [[NEXT_LINE]] 3rd Fly on a true course of 345° to regain his original course.

questions.question: Referring to question #21, if the Concorde could maintain this for the entire 36,400 kilometer journey around the world (with calm winds everywhere), how long would this journey take?
questions.context: Referenced question 21: The Concorde is traveling at Mach 2.2, and the outside air temperature is -76°F. What is this true airspeed in statute miles per hour?

questions.question: Without any calculations, you assume a time and distance to climb and descend for Route 1 total 25 minutes and 125 nautical miles, how long does it take to complete this route? Zero wind.
questions.context: You are flying for a corporation that has many corporate aircraft. On two separate days in two separate aircraft (a G IV and King Air), you are asked to fly from Madison, WI (800 feet MSL) to Rapid City, SD (2200 feet MSL). You fly in a standard atmosphere unless otherwise stated. Route 1 is with the G IV and route 2 is with the King Air and are as follows: Route 1 MSN to RAP direct (602 nautical miles) [[NEXT_LINE]] True Course = 287° G IV burns 2000 pounds of Jet A per hour and travels at Mach 0.77 (-45°C) Route 2 Leg 1 (MSN to DSM), Leg 2 (DSM to RAP) 294 nautical miles and 422 nautical miles respectively [[NEXT_LINE]] True Course = Leg 1 (248°) and Leg 2 (322°) King Air burns 750 pounds of Jet A per hour and travels at 230 knots, true airspeed Des Moines, IA (600 feet MSL) is a landing airport.

questions.question: If the winds aloft switch to 115° at 15 knots, giving him a groundspeed of 325 km/hr, what is the total flight time for the entire trip?
questions.context: Referenced question 1: Tim is flying from General Mitchell International (MKE) in Milwaukee, WI to Kalamazoo/Battle Creek Regional (AZO) in Battle Creek, MI a distance of 281 nautical miles. His indicated airspeed is 142 knots at 7500 ft pressure altitude, on a true course of 094°, with the winds aloft of 160° at 25 knots. If Tim's altimeter setting is 29.99" and the OAT is 15°C, what is Tim's groundspeed? [[DIVIDER]] Use information from problem 1. Tim decides to over-fly AZO and return to MKE.

questions.question: 876540 seconds is the same as:
In cruise flight at Flight Level 220, the outside air temperature is -22°C, if you are cruising at 0.545 Mach, how long will it take to fly 437 sm (assuming no winds)?
How long does it take to travel the original routing?
questions.context: At 7:30 am, Tim is departing Dallas-Fort Worth (1000 ft MSL) for Portland, OR (0 ft MSL). His routing essentially takes him direct to Denver Intl. (DIA), 900 nautical miles, then direct PDX, an additional 1150 statute miles. ATC allows him the option to fly direct to PDX, 1400 nautical miles. The pressure everywhere is 29.92" Hg. Climb and descent winds are calm. Cruise winds aloft = 280@80 knots. His performance is as follows: [[NEXT_LINE]] Climb True Airspeed = 360 knots, [[NEXT_LINE]] Cruise Airspeed 0.79 Mach, and [[NEXT_LINE]] Descent True Airspeed = 470 knots. Temperature at FL390 is -35°C. True Course DFW to DIA is 340°, True Course DIA to PDX is 295°, and True Course DFW to PDX is 315°. Rate of Climb is 1000 fpm and Rate of Descent is 1500 fpm.

questions.question: How long does it take to travel the new routing?
